free_text: Experimental Investigation of the Resonance Self-
Shielding and Doppler Effect in Uranium and Tantalum
beam energy: 45 to 70 MeV. Repetition rate
(pulse/sec), Pulse width: 20 ns. Nominal beam power:
450 to 980 W. Primary neutron production target:
Tantalum. Neutron source position in moderator:
Dimension (thickness, height x width x depth,...):
Updated to new date formats, lower case.
BP: Replaced the previously listed cross
section data for U and Ta with the transmission and
SIR data from the RPI thesis. Corrected reaction
strings from cross sections to transmission and self
resolution data supplied by Prof. R.C.Block.
Corrected author list, changed subentry 11 from
transmission to SIF, otherwise it is a duplicate of
OS. Data point correction in sub.4;
remark on thickness added to SAMPLE in subs.5-10,
A 12.7-mm-thick 10B4C slab in the beam that is
viewed by two 203.2-mm-dia. by 50.8-mm thick NaI
crystals. Surface Dimensions: 101.6 mm x 101.6 mm.
thermodynamic temperature was 293+-2 K, and effective

shape determined from a separate measurement of

energy resolution of 10% can be assumed for all the
